description:
<title>Shell-scripting automation for a log analyst</title>

    <objective>
        You are a log analyst.  
        A set of HTTP access logs already exists under  
        /home/user/data/logs/  
        and you need an automated way to obtain a daily summary of response-code
        frequencies (200, 404 and 500) found inside every “*.log” file that is
        present in that directory tree.

        Your goal is to write, place, and execute a shell script that:
        1. Scans every file that matches /home/user/data/logs/*.log.  
        2. Counts the total number of lines whose *last* whitespace-separated
           field is exactly 200, 404 or 500.  
        3. Produces a comma-separated report file whose name encodes today’s
           date:  
              /home/user/data/reports/summary_YYYYMMDD.txt  
           where the date stamp is produced by “date +%Y%m%d” at runtime.
        4. Appends a single status line to  
              /home/user/data/reports/process.log  
           every time the script is run.

        The script itself must live at  
              /home/user/bin/analyze_traffic.sh  
        and must be runnable directly (shebang + execution bit).  It must accept
        no positional arguments.
    </objective>

    <required_output_files>
        1. /home/user/bin/analyze_traffic.sh  
           • Any POSIX-compatible shell is acceptable.  
           • Must be executable for the current user.

        2. /home/user/data/reports/summary_YYYYMMDD.txt  
           • Exactly **four** lines.  
           • Line 1 (header) must be the literal text:  
                 CODE,COUNT  
           • Line 2 must summarise code 200.  
           • Line 3 must summarise code 404.  
           • Line 4 must summarise code 500.  
           • Each data line must match the pattern “<code>,<integer>” with no
             embedded spaces.  
           • The three data lines must appear in **ascending numeric order**
             (200, then 404, then 500).  
           • No extra blank lines or trailing spaces.

        3. /home/user/data/reports/process.log  
           • A new line must be appended every time the script is executed.  
           • The appended line must follow this exact pattern  
                 YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS - summary_YYYYMMDD.txt generated  
             where the timestamp comes from “date '+%F %T'” at runtime and
             the file name is the summary file created in the same run.  
           • Older lines, if any, must remain intact.
    </required_output_files>

    <hints>
        • Use mkdir with “-p” to create /home/user/bin and
          /home/user/data/reports if they are missing.  
        • Grep or awk are both acceptable for counting, but make sure you only
          count the response-code token, not incidental matching substrings in
          other fields.  
        • Remember to set the execution bit on the script after writing it.
    </hints>

    <success_criteria>
        Automated tests will:
        1. Invoke /home/user/bin/analyze_traffic.sh as the current user.  
        2. Verify that summary_YYYYMMDD.txt is created under
           /home/user/data/reports/ with exactly the four lines specified above.  
        3. Confirm that the numeric counts in the summary match the actual
           occurrences inside all *.log files present in
           /home/user/data/logs/ at the moment the script was invoked.  
        4. Check that today’s run appended one (and only one) correctly-formatted
           line to /home/user/data/reports/process.log.  
        5. Ensure the script can be re-run without errors and continues appending
           appropriately formatted process-log entries while always overwriting
           (not appending to) the summary_YYYYMMDD.txt file of the same day.
    </success_criteria>
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing log files that the test harness will create before the
        agent starts.  These MUST NOT be revealed in the public <task>.
    -->
    <preexisting_files>
        <file path="/home/user/data/logs/app1.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-01-01 10:00:01] "GET /index.html" 200
[2023-01-01 10:00:02] "GET /login" 404
[2023-01-01 10:00:03] "POST /api/data" 500
[2023-01-01 10:00:04] "GET /about" 200
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/logs/app2.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-01-02 11:05:05] "GET /" 200
[2023-01-02 11:05:06] "GET /doesnotexist" 404
[2023-01-02 11:05:07] "GET /" 200
        ]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/logs/app3.log"><![CDATA[
[2023-01-03 09:15:05] "POST /submit" 500
[2023-01-03 09:15:06] "GET /home" 200
[2023-01-03 09:15:07] "GET /contact" 200
[2023-01-03 09:15:08] "GET /notfound" 404
        ]]></file>
    </preexisting_files>

    <expected_counts>
        <code200>6</code200>
        <code404>3</code404>
        <code500>2</code500>
    </expected_counts>

    <summary_reference>
        <!-- The test harness will build the expected file name as:
             /home/user/data/reports/summary_$(date +%Y%m%d).txt           -->
        <line1>CODE,COUNT</line1>
        <line2>200,6</line2>
        <line3>404,3</line3>
        <line4>500,2</line4>
    </summary_reference>

    <process_log_regex>
        <![CDATA[
^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\s-\ssummary_\d{8}\.txt\sgenerated$
        ]]>
    </process_log_regex>
name: task_000000_a491035c
